_comment: Cross-implementation codec conformance corpus, read by BOTH the Rust crate (crates/pl-graph-core, conformance test) and the TS @pl-graph/serialization suite. Compare graphs STRUCTURALLY (normalize: sort nodes/edges, labels as sets, props sorted by key, edge ids ignored) — NOT bytewise: edge ids (Rust 'e{index}' vs TS rando) and property/label key ordering legitimately differ between the two engines. `canonical` is an NDJSON document exercising multi-label nodes, int/float/bool/string/list props, edge props, and an empty-prop node; every codec must round-trip it on each side. `reject` lists malformed inputs that BOTH engines must reject with the same ErrorCode (only behaviours verified aligned across the parity work are listed here — null-property and numeric-edge-id handling and the like are intentionally omitted because they still differ).
_canonical_normal_comment: The committed golden normal form of `canonical` (the normalizer both sides apply). BOTH engines must produce this exact string when they decode `canonical`, which is what guarantees they interpret it identically — not merely that each round-trips within itself.
